---
title: "OLTP vs. OLAP"
engine: knitr
filters:
  - interactive-duckdb
databases:
  - name: cookbook
    path: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Alaburda/data-analyst-sql-cookbook/master/databases/cookbook.duckdb"
    format: duckdb
---

# OLAP vs OLTP

Why do you need both, what's the difference, differences between rowstore, columnstore and row groups.

```{r setup}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(duckdb)

con <- dbConnect(duckdb(), "../databases/cookbook.duckdb", read_only = TRUE)

```

Why generally neither architecture be relevant to you because you are doing table scans anyway lol. OLAP as an engine optimised for fact table data, OLTP for transactional data.
